title: Drinking Wine and Intoxicants
subtitle: The Mother of Evils — Forbidden as an Abomination from Satan

verses.arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِنَّمَا الْخَمْرُ وَالْمَيْسِرُ وَالْأَنصَابُ وَالْأَزْلَامُ رِجْسٌ مِّنْ عَمَلِ الشَّيْطَانِ فَاجْتَنِبُوهُ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ
verses.translation: O you who have believed, indeed, intoxicants, gambling, [sacrificing on] stone alters [to other than Allah], and divining arrows are but defilement from the work of Satan, so avoid it that you may be successful.

verses.arabic: إِنَّمَا يُرِيدُ الشَّيْطَانُ أَن يُوقِعَ بَيْنَكُمُ الْعَدَاوَةَ وَالْبَغْضَاءَ فِي الْخَمْرِ وَالْمَيْسِرِ وَيَصُدَّكُمْ عَن ذِكْرِ اللَّهِ وَعَنِ الصَّلَاةِ ۖ فَهَلْ أَنتُم مُّنتَهُونَ
verses.translation: Satan only wants to cause between you animosity and hatred through intoxicants and gambling and to avert you from the remembrance of Allah and from prayer. So will you not desist?

verses.arabic: يَسْأَلُونَكَ عَنِ الْخَمْرِ وَالْمَيْسِرِ ۖ قُلْ فِيهِمَا إِثْمٌ كَبِيرٌ وَمَنَافِعُ لِلنَّاسِ وَإِثْمُهُمَا أَكْبَرُ مِن نَّفْعِهِمَا
verses.translation: They ask you about wine and gambling. Say, 'In them is great sin and [yet, some] benefit for people. But their sin is greater than their benefit.'

brief_explanation: The Quran classifies khamr (wine and all intoxicants) as rijs — defilement from the work of Satan — placing it in a category alongside idolatry and divination. The divine reasoning is explicit: Satan uses intoxicants to plant enmity and hatred between people and to turn them away from the remembrance of Allah and from prayer. When a person is intoxicated, every act of worship becomes invalid, every inhibition falls, and every sin becomes accessible — making khamr the 'mother of evils' as the scholars described it. The Quran's prohibition came in stages, acknowledging that its harm outweighs any perceived benefit, before the final complete and absolute prohibition.
capital_punishment: The hadd for drinking khamr is 40 lashes according to Imam Malik and Imam Ahmad, and 80 lashes according to the Hanafi and Shafi'i schools, with the 80-lash opinion based on the practice of Umar ibn al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with him). This applies to a Muslim adult who drinks voluntarily. There is no execution prescribed specifically for drinking — it is a lashing hadd. If the person repeats the offense, the hadd is repeated. A person who declares khamr to be lawful is considered to have committed kufr, and the ruling of apostasy would then apply.
